title: 2.3 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lesson 2.3.
topic_path: History of the Universe and Earth

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is the end of the plasma Universe important when it comes to creating greater complexity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Universe cooled enough to allow for the formation of atoms.
Energy could no longer be created or destroyed.
The fundamental force, gravity, was created.
Human life began at the end of the plasma Universe, 380,000 years after the Big Bang.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When protons fuse during the formation of stars, which of the following is released in massive quantities?
**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**Why is the formation of stars an important threshold in Big History?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Stars created the “hot spots” necessary for further complexity.
Stars rid the Universe of dark matter.
Stars created cosmic microwave background radiation (CMBR).
Stars exploded during the Big Bang.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What has been an important tool in discovering what existed before the formation of stars and how stars themselves formed?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]
